economic_notes: First Nation has investments in Halfway River Holdings Limited Partnership (holding/treasury entity), Halfway River Holdings Ltd., Halfway River Ventures GP Ltd., and Six Nations Ventures Limited Partnership (16.67% interest, log sort yard, contract hauling and janitorial services in Fort St. John area). Corporate restructuring occurred October 1, 2020. Earnings from investment in First Nation partnerships and business entities: $7,563,350. Construction projects in progress include meat plant, health centre, housing, water system upgrade, ambulance bay. Specific claim filed in BC Supreme Court under Treaty 8. Gas station decommissioning contingent liability.
notes: Audit qualified due to non-disclosure of budget information as required by PSAB (Note 14). Four entities accounted for under modified equity method (GBEs): Six Nations Ventures LP, Halfway River Ventures GP Ltd., Halfway River Holdings Ltd., Halfway River Holdings Limited Partnership. Corporate restructuring occurred October 1, 2020. Deferred revenue of $14.85M is very large relative to total revenue, largely from industry agreements. Ottawa Trust Funds (capital and revenue) of $3,468,539 held by Government of Canada included in accumulated surplus. Specific claim filed in BC Supreme Court under Treaty 8 with promissory notes payable of $3,323,685 to Crown. Potential impairment of sewer upgrade costs and gas station decommissioning liability not quantified. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC ($2,076,034) + Province of BC ($3,650,377) + CMHC ($672,559) + First Nations Health Authority ($639,778) = $7,038,748. OSR includes oil and gas, industry agreements, gaming revenue sharing, rental, interest, donations, other revenue, and equity earnings.
